---
title: Projection of tumour cells on developing hindbrain with SingleR
date: 2025-02-18
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To determine the MB tumour's closest cell type using RL lineage cells as a reference.


## Files and directories

### Code

The code can be found in `HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/01_project_on_dev_ref.R`. All code was run using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment.

Briefly, the script takes the following files as input:

- the Vladoiu scRNA-seq MB tumour dataset with 8 samples; the tumour cells will be annotated using the cell types in the reference dataset
- the reference datasets:
  - the integrated Aldinger + Sepp human RL lineage cells (performed by Shraddha)
  - the UBCs that were subset from the dataset above (performed by Shraddha)

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  tumour_dataset[("Vladoiu\ntumour dataset")]
  rl_dataset[("Aldinger+Sepp\nintegrated RL lineage")]
  ubc_dataset[("Aldinger+Sepp\nintegrated UBC subclusters")]
  r_script["01_project_on_dev_ref.R"]
  rl_ref["SingleR with RL reference"]
  ubc_ref["subset UBCs and re-run\nSingleR on UBC subclusters"]
  merged_ref["SingleR with merged\nRL/UBC labels as reference"]
  rl_only["output to:\nfull_RL_lineage"]
  rl_then_ubc["output to:\nfull_RL_then_UBC"]
  merged["output to:\nmerged_RL_with_UBC"]
  tumour_dataset --> r_script
  rl_dataset --> r_script
  ubc_dataset --> r_script
  r_script --> rl_ref
  rl_ref --> ubc_ref
  r_script --> merged_ref
  rl_ref ---> rl_only
  ubc_ref --> rl_then_ubc
  merged_ref ---> merged
```

### Output

The output can be found in `<pailab projects>/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/`. The bash script that was used to call the R script can also be found in this folder.

Results are located in three output folders - see flowchart above. Each folder contains:

- `prediction_results.rds`: output of `SingleR` with the score and assigned label for each cell in the tumour dataset
- `score_heatmap.png`: `plotScoreHeatmap` showing each tumour cell's score for each reference developmental cell type
- `annot_bar.png`: Bar plots showing the distribution of tumour cells
- `umaps.png`: UMAP of the tumour cells
- `delta_distr.png`: `plotDeltaDistribution` showing the delta score for each cell, i.e., the difference between the score for the assigned label and the median score of all other predictions
- `score.distr.png`: `plotScoreDistribution` showing the distribution of scores for each assigned label


## Results

Note that SingleR was run using Shraddha's original UBC clustering results. Quang is re-running the UBC clustering, so the script will need to be re-run to generate the UBC results. The results here are only an example of the script's output.

### Using the RL lineage as a reference

The figures below show the results from using the general RL lineage as the reference (with UBCs as one cluster).

![Heatmap of SingleR scores for each of the tumour cells. Annotations above the heatplot show the tumour cell's final assigned cell type and its tumour subtype.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/full_RL_lineage/score_heatmap.png){#fig-rl-only-heatmap}

![Proportion of MB tumour cells by subtype, sample, or cluster. Colours indicate the Seurat cluster (top) or the cell type annotation as predicted by SingleR (middle and bottom).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/full_RL_lineage/annot_bar.png){#fig-rl-only-bar}

![UMAP of the tumour cells coloured by sample (left), subtype (middle), and SingleR-assigned cell type (right).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/full_RL_lineage/umaps.png){#fig-rl-only-umap}

### Splitting the UBC into clusters

The figures below show the results after taking the MB tumour cells that were assigned the UBC label and re-running SingleR with the UBC subclusters.

![Heatmap of SingleR scores for each of the tumour cells. Annotations above the heatplot show the tumour cell's final assigned cell type and its tumour subtype.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/full_RL_then_UBC/score_heatmap.png){#fig-rl-then-ubc-heatmap}

![Proportion of MB tumour cells by subtype, sample, or cluster. Colours indicate the Seurat cluster (top) or the cell type annotation as predicted by SingleR (middle and bottom).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/full_RL_then_UBC/annot_bar.png){#fig-rl-then-ubc-bar}

![UMAP of the tumour cells coloured by sample (left), subtype (middle), and SingleR-assigned cell type (right).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/full_RL_then_UBC/umaps.png){#fig-rl-then-ubc-umap}

### Merging RL lineage and UBC subclusters

The figures below show the results from combining the RL lineage and UBC subcluster labels to use as the reference, and running SingleR on the merged cell type annotations.

![Heatmap of SingleR scores for each of the tumour cells. Annotations above the heatplot show the tumour cell's final assigned cell type and its tumour subtype.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/merged_RL_with_UBC/score_heatmap.png){#fig-merged-heatmap}

![Proportion of MB tumour cells by subtype, sample, or cluster. Colours indicate the Seurat cluster (top) or the cell type annotation as predicted by SingleR (middle and bottom).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/merged_RL_with_UBC/annot_bar.png){#fig-merged-bar}

![UMAP of the tumour cells coloured by sample (left), subtype (middle), and SingleR-assigned cell type (right).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250218/merged_RL_with_UBC/umaps.png){#fig-merged-umap}
